title: Wildcard delete/nullify (*)
text: Wildcard delete/nullify (*) Delete or nullify a set of records. Description The connector is responsible for any necessary locking. Table 1. Modes Mode Description Mode 0 Delete all records identified by the profile in the ‘if’ list or where structure. Mode 1 Update the foreign key fields to NULL (the native missing value symbol of the DBMS) for all records identified by the value of the foreign key fields in the ‘if’ list. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—The table that Wildcard is requested for is open. Table 2. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled. uctrl->udrv Points to the connector workspace if a workspace was requested in the Info request. uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. uctrl->ufldlst Field list that describes all the fields in the table. uctrl->uiflst Identifies the record to be updated. uctrl->uwlist Identifies the records to be deleted or nullified for ISAM-like DBMSs. To Uniface Table 3. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Wildcard delete/nullify was successful. -3 Exceptional error. -9 I/O server logon error. -16 DBMS reported network error. When the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . Service Functions uhitchk —checks if a record matches the specified selection profile. unullify —sets the foreign key fields in the iflist to NULL. uready —use with an ISAM-like DBMS in combination with uhitchk , to stop searching through the preferred index. TM-Controlled Connectors TM-controlled DBMS connectors are not allowed to begin, commit, or rollback transactions. Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
